name: Sweet-Sour Kabobs
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the green onions into 2-inch pieces.
2. Cut the green pepper into 1-inch pieces after seeding.
3. Drain the pineapple slices and reserve the syrup for later use in the recipe.
4. Mix the meat with 1 tablespoon soy sauce. Shape the mixture into 36 meatballs.
5. Place the meatballs in a glass bowl or plastic bag.
6. Mix the reserved pineapple syrup, brown sugar, vinegar, and 2 tablespoons soy sauce until the sugar is dissolved. Pour over the meatballs and refrigerate for at least 3 hours.
7. Drain the marinade from the meatballs into a small saucepan, stir in the cornstarch. Cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and boils. Boil and stir for 1 minute. Remove the sauce from the heat and set aside.
8. Cut the pineapple slices into quarters.
9. On each of 6 metal skewers, alternate 6 meatballs with pineapple and vegetables.
10. Brush the kabobs with part of the sauce.
11. Set the oven control to broil and/or 550 degrees F. Broil kabobs 4-inches from the heat until desired doneness, about 15 to 20 minutes. Brush occasionally with the sauce and gently push with a fork to turn.
